words.def: a chemical element. Carbon is found in all living things, existing in a pure state as diamond and graphite.
words.example: carbon fibre

words.def: all the people who act in a play or film
words.example: an all-star cast (= including many well-known actors)

words.def: not formal
words.example: casual clothes (= comfortable clothes that you choose to wear in your free time)

words.def: an act of catching something, for example a ball
words.example: Roger made some brilliant catches at today's game.

words.def: the person or thing that makes something happen
words.example: Unemployment is a major cause of poverty.

words.def: a large hole in the side of a hill or cliff or under the ground
words.example: the mouth (= the entrance) of the cave

words.def: the top inside surface of a room
words.example: a large room with a high ceiling

words.def: the smallest unit of living matter that can exist on its own. All plants and animals are made up of cells.
words.example: red and white blood cells

words.def: the state of being certain
words.example: There is no certainty that the president's removal would end the civil war.

words.def: an official document that may be used to prove that the facts it states are true
words.example: a birth/marriage/death certificate

words.def: a series of connected metal rings, used for pulling or fastening things; a length of chain used for a particular purpose
words.example: She wore a heavy gold chain around her neck.

words.def: a piece of furniture for one person to sit on, with a back, a seat and four legs
words.example: a table and chairs

words.def: the person in charge of a meeting, who tells people when they can speak, etc.
words.example: Sir Herbert took it upon himself to act as chairman.

words.def: a new or difficult task that tests somebody’s ability and skill
words.example: a tough/major/significant challenge

words.def: difficult in an interesting way that tests your ability
words.example: challenging work/questions/problems

words.def: a competition to find the best player or team in a particular sport
words.example: the National Basketball Association Championship

words.def: very typical of something or of somebody’s character
words.example: characteristic of something/somebody, Community support of families is characteristic of many societies.

words.def: very pleasant or attractive
words.example: The cottage is tiny, but it's charming.

words.def: a diagram, lists of figures, etc. that shows information
words.example: Some shares have lost two-thirds of their value since being issued (see chart).

words.def: an act of running or driving after somebody/something in order to catch them or it
words.example: The thieves were caught by police after a short chase.

words.def: a person who cheats, especially in a game
words.example: You little cheat!

words.def: either side of the face below the eyes
words.example: chubby/rosy/pink cheeks

words.def: a shout of joy, support or praise
words.example: A great cheer went up from the crowd.

words.def: most important
words.example: the chief cause/problem/reason

words.def: a long thin piece of potato fried in oil or fat
words.example: He was eating a burger and chips.

words.def: a group of people who sing together, for example in church services or public performances
words.example: She sings in the school choir.

words.def: to cut something into pieces with a sharp tool such as a knife
words.example: chop something, He was chopping logs for firewood.

words.def: a line, route or journey around a place
words.example: The race ended with eight laps of a city centre circuit.

words.def: the conditions and facts that are connected with and affect a situation, an event or an action
words.example: Police said there were no suspicious circumstances surrounding the boy's death.

words.def: to mention something as a reason or an example, or in order to support what you are saying
words.example: cite something, She cited examples of companies the city has helped relocate or expand.

words.def: a person who has the legal right to belong to a particular country
words.example: The defeat of the president did not change the lives of ordinary citizens for the better.

words.def: connected with the people who live in a country
words.example: civil unrest (= that is caused by groups of people within a country)

words.def: a state of human society that is very developed and organized
words.example: the technology of modern civilization

words.def: to make something clearer or easier to understand
words.example: clarify something, to clarify a situation/problem/issue

words.def: accepted or deserving to be accepted as one of the best or most important of its kind
words.example: a classic film/story/car/game

words.def: to arrange something in groups according to features that they have in common
words.example: The books in the library are classified according to subject.

words.def: a group of words that includes a subject and a verb, and forms a sentence or part of a sentence
words.example: in a clause, There are languages that require the subject to come before the object in a clause.

words.def: a person whose job is to serve customers in a shop
words.example: The clerk at the counter gave me too little change.

words.def: a person who uses the services or advice of a professional person or organization
words.example: She's a well-known lawyer with many famous clients.

words.def: a high area of rock with a very steep side, often at the edge of the sea or ocean
words.example: the cliff edge/top

words.def: a building or part of a hospital where people can go for special medical treatment or advice
words.example: Your local family planning clinic can give you advice about birth control.

words.def: a short part of a film that is shown separately
words.example: Here is a clip from her latest movie.

words.def: near in space or time
words.example: I had no idea the beach was so close.

words.def: near in space or time
words.example: He walked into the room, closely followed by the rest of the family.

words.def: clothes, especially a particular type of clothes
words.example: Workers at the factory wear protective clothing.

words.def: the fact of two things happening at the same time by chance, in a surprising way
words.example: a strange/an extraordinary/a remarkable coincidence

words.def: a sudden failure of something, such as an institution, a business or a course of action
words.example: the collapse of the Soviet Union

words.def: a person that you work with, especially in a profession or a business
words.example: work/senior/male colleagues

words.def: a person who collects things, either as a hobby or as a job
words.example: a stamp collector

words.def: a country or an area that is governed by people from another, more powerful, country
words.example: former British colonies

words.def: full of bright colours or having a lot of different colours
words.example: colourful shop windows

words.def: two or more things joined or mixed together to form a single unit
words.example: combination of something, The tragedy was due to a combination of factors.

words.def: the state of being physically relaxed and free from pain; the state of having a pleasant life, with everything that you need
words.example: The hotel offers a high standard of comfort and service.

words.def: humorous and making you laugh
words.example: a comic monologue/story

words.def: an order given to a person or an animal
words.example: Begin when I give the command.

words.def: a person who is in charge of something, especially an officer in charge of a particular group of soldiers or a military operation
words.example: military/allied/field/flight commanders

words.def: an official group of people who have been given responsibility to control something, or to find out about something, usually for the government
words.example: The commission is expected to report its findings next month.

words.def: a promise to do something or to behave in a particular way; a promise to support somebody/something; the fact of committing yourself
words.example: I'm not ready to make a long-term commitment.

words.def: a group of people who are chosen, usually by a larger group, to make decisions or to deal with a particular subject
words.example: an executive/advisory/organizing committee

words.def: usually; very often; by most people
words.example: Christopher is commonly known as Kit.

words.def: all the people who live in a particular area, country, etc. when talked about as a group
words.example: The local community supported us from the start.

words.def: measured or judged by how similar or different it is to something else
words.example: Then he was living in comparative comfort (= compared with others or with his own life at a previous time).
